post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 201
Procrastination Mafia

Welcome to Nonexistent Angels Inc., saving humanity from itself since 6000 BC. Our existential purpose is to sit on people's shoulders, watching them screw up spectacularly for no reason whatsoever. We do absolutely nothing as people indulge in their seemingly insignificant vices, and when their lives inevitably implode as a result, we console them with nonexistent comfort as if we never saw it coming. Our resume is filled with resounding successes where we totally did not nudge humanity away from some of its worst mistakes, encompassing everything from slavery to Trump to The Emoji Movie. As you can see, the services that we provide are utterly indispensable, halting disasters in their tracks one bit of procrastination at a time.

Turns out though, even nonexistent angels can fall . . .

Important Rules
  1. This list is just a summary of the most important and/or unusual rules. For experienced players, simply reading this along with the setup tab may be sufficient.
  2. You may not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  3. Each "day" phase in this game is 2 weeks in real life while "night" lasts 3 days.
  4. Do not post during night, especially during Night 0.
  5. When voting, you must format them correctly. If your vote isn't counted, there is a good chance you messed up the format. Incorrect capitalizations of usernames, as well as capital i/lowercase L, are not differentiated and will count as valid votes.
  6. Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2 until LYLO.
  7. Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead. A dead QT may be created upon request.
  8. This game is a no-action Night 0 start with a dawn phase, so Day 1 will not start until we make sure everyone knows the rules, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say holy moly, good lord, oh my god, or some variation of that in the thread.
  9. All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable.
  10. Use common sense.
  11. All other rules are standard. However, if you break a rule that isn't covered here, you will still be warned.
  12. Best of luck, and hope you have fun!

  • Rules
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas amongst others for the rule template.

    • Make sure you are reasonably aware of all standard mafia game rules and follow them.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread. A dead QT may be created upon request.
    • Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • Do not abuse loopholes.

  • Voting and Hanging
    • This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the most votes at the deadline will be hanged.
    • If two players have the same amount of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that amount of votes first will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, which will result in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and "Vote: fDAs" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames. There is one exception to this; see below.
    • For the purpose of voting, l (lowercase L) and I (uppercase i) are identical.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags will not be counted.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. For instance, do not reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, it is recommended that you give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
    • You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by saying "Vote: No Hang".
    • If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority. This applies only to a no hang.
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • All times and deadlines will be given in times currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day will be 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 72 hours (3 days) after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence), they will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 36-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be force replaced.
    • If a player is going to be in V/LA, they must post in bold in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language.
    • Do not make any personal attacks.
    • Do not post excessive spam.
    • Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it harder for them to read and play may be a punishable offenses, at the mods' discretion.
    •  Follow all of the rules, including AoPS community rules.
    •  Be mature.
    • Use common sense.
    •  For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.

    In addition to these guidelines, please note the category of posts which are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are now liable to be warned for these posts. Please note that this should have little to no effect on normal player behavior: this enforcement policy has been created to better define the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to discourage negatively received posts. Please refer to the following tab for details.

    Discouraged Behavior
    The following are examples of posts that may result in a warning:

    • Posts with falsified text that imitates the text that indicates when a post has been edited.
    • Posts that are blank, or otherwise contain no content.
    • Unsolicited advertising.
    • Multiple posts with identical content.
    • Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text.
    • Extended serious communication in code or non-English languages.

    This list may be updated frequently, in which case a notice will be posted in the announcement section in the bottom of the OP.

    Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met:

    1. The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior in this game
    2. The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content
    3. The posts have caused significantly negative reactions from other players

    Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines or receive a warning even if they break them. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rules.

  • Moderators
    • mathjay and HannahW are the moderators of Mafia Game 201.
    • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for votecounts, activity checks, confirm counts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will still either be announced or noted at the bottom of the OP. Most rule changes will be designed to have as little effect on normal, day-to-day play as possible and will instead be designed to streamline gameplay. However, it is recommended that players occasionally update themselves on rule changes.
    • The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules.

    MG201 OP is based on mathjay formatted ruleset (MFR) version 2.

Setup
This game is based on the Pick Your Poison setup on MafiaScum, originally designed by JDodge. It is an open 13p setup where, before town roles are distributed, the mafia can choose which three town PRs will play in the game. Mafia are therefore aware of which PRs are in the game, but they are not made aware of the identities of the players who are the PRs.

The five possible town PRs are:
  • Innocent Child
  • Tracker
  • 1-Shot Cop
  • Jailkeeper
  • 2-Shot Vigilante

There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is a Night 0 start. Although the mafia will not be able to kill during Night 0, they will have access to their QuickTopic in order to discuss and choose the three town PRs.
  • Approximately 72 hours after the start of Night 0, the game will move onto Dawn. All Role PMs will have been distributed, and players will be allowed to post. When everyone confirms, Day 1 will start and the deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Detailed information about pregame and Night 0 can be found under "RNG Process."
  • There are no Night 0 actions.
  • Cop and Tracker results are sane.
  • Jailkeeper may not jail themselves.
  • Vigilante may not shoot twice in a single Night.
  • Mafia have Daychat; they may discuss in their Quicktopic during both Day and Night while they are alive.

RNG Process
Upon signing up for this game, players will receive the following PM:
PM wrote:
Hello, [player], and welcome to Mafia Game 201!

This is your Nonexistent Angels Inc. agent communication portal. This post will be updated shortly with your assignment and other relevant administrative messages.

Once signups have been completed, the PM will be updated with the following message:
PM wrote:
Hello!

You seem to be having an identity crisis. Hold tight while we figure out whether you're evil or not.
Ahem.
I mean, please hold while we figure out your deployment status and assign your positions.

Due to the special mechanics involved in this game, your Role PM has been delayed.
  • If you are a member of the Mafia, your Role PM will be sent in 24 hours, at which point you will have 72 hours to choose the 3 Town PRs with your teammates.
  • If you are a member of the Town, your Role PM will be sent in 96 hours, at which point Dawn will start.

Best,
mathjay and HannahW
Angelic Resources Department
Nonexistent Angles Incorporated

Three mafia goons will be chosen via RNG (Random Number Generator) from a list of players ordered first by descending rank and then by alphabetical order.

Using the List Randomizer on random.org, a list of 1-10 Town and 11-13 Mafia will be randomized and applied to the ordered playerlist in order to determine player alignments.

At this point, PMs will be sent to the chosen Mafia members, who will be given 72 hours to choose the 3 town PRs that will be in this game. Once the 72 hours are up, the roles will be distributed using the List Randomizer in a similar method, with 1-7 VTs and 8-10 PRs. The PRs will be listed in an order consistent with the order they were in in the setup tab. After the roles are determined, all remaining role PMs will be sent out and Dawn will start.

Role PMs
Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Nonexistent Regular Angel (Vanilla Townie). You too were a procrastinating human once, but you have since ascended into extant nonexistence. Now, as an agent of Nonexistent Angels Inc., you strive to prevent others from following your foolish footsteps, although you have no special powers other than your ability to silently laugh at human folly. Oh, and you can talk and vote, although that doesn't mean you'll be heard, necessarily. If a nonexistent angel talks but no one can hear, did it even talk?

You win when all members of mafia faction are eliminated.
Innocent Townie Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Nightwinged Angel (Innocent Townie). You genuinely believe in Nonexistent Angel Inc.'s mission to better humanity and try your best, because that's the sort of angel you are, nonexistence be darned. In your spare time, you weaponize your bright grins and snuggle hugs to make friends with absolutely anyone and everyone, all powered by the power of pure, unadulterated sugary cereal. Everyone trusts you, and even if they didn't, well. You don't like pulling the Rich, Powerful Dad card, but you're not afraid of using it if that's what it'll take to prove your innocence and pull off this mission.

Once during the game, at any time you wish, you may contact the mods asking for your innocence to be verified. You will be then announced as an Innocent Townie in the thread, confirming you as a member of the town.

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated.
Tracker Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Pipin' Hot Tea Angel (Tracker), otherwise known as @AngelTMZ on Angel Twitter. You absolutely adore tea. You live (and die) for gossip. Actually, you actually have lived and died for gossip, an' you almost went to hell because of it, a fact you are somewhat proud of. Your eyes, ears, and trusty smartphone are still as legendary (and feared too) as they were when you were human, and you run the bestest grapevine in heaven. If anything anywhere remotely interesting happens, you betcha you know about it. Stalker, who moi? Pshaw. Shush and spill the tea boiiiiii. Ayyyyy.

Each night, you may choose a player to track. At the end of the night, you will be informed who your target visited, if any. If the target does not use a night action, or if you are roleblocked, you will receive "No gossip" as your result.

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated.
1-Shot Cop Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Gloriole Angel (1-Shot Cop). The angel of the halo. That's it. Nothing else. Please and thank you. Just like poor old Caen, you are just thoroughly done. You will never admit this, but you for one are very relieved that you are actually nonexistent, and you have zero sympathy for the unrefined heathens bumbling on below. The only reason you're working for Nonexistent Angels Inc. is because... well, it's better than being nonemployed on top of nonexistent. At least you found a use for your halo again; who knew determining whether an angel was of pure of heart would ever be a thing?

Once during the game, during night, you may choose a player to cop. You will receive their alignment. You may use this ability only once during the entire game.

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated.
Jailkeeper Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Angel of Kaen (Jailkeeper). You are old, and your name used to be spelled differently, but you're not sure anymore because the humans seem to have some kind of obsession with spelling it wrong. Kayn, Cain, Kane, Cayn, Kanye, god, you really don't know anymore. At this point, you are just done, and you're not entirely sure why you're working for Nonexistent Angels Inc. You'll still help though. Your Mark may not work on humans, but it works on your fellow angels just fine, though a bit... different. Hopefully it'll still be enough for some peace and quiet around here. Is a nap without something going wrong too much to ask for?

Each night, you may choose a player to jail. For that night, they will be unable to die and unable to use their night actions if they have one. Any other investigative roles targeting your target will still receive results, however. You are also not allowed to jail yourself.

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated.
2-Shot Vigilante Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Angel With a Shotgun (2-Shot Vigilante). You love calling shotgun. You love listening the song shotgun. You just love any and all shotguns and must've inherited that love from the Life Before when you were a mere foolish human bean because you haven't seen a lot of other gun-happy Nonexistent Regular Angels. Hey at least you'll be putting your trusty rusty nonexistent shotgun to good use on this mission, eh?

Each night, you may choose a player to kill. You have two shots, but you may not take more than one (pot)shot each night.

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated.
Mafia Goon Role PM wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are an Employee of Abaddon Industries (Mafia Goon) and psst, guess what, you're evil now! I - Phudoss - have bonked the procrastinating idiots in charge of Angel Resources and have sneakily taken over the entire department. I am hereby declaring you a wonderful traitor to this corporate disaster of a trainwreck and ordering you to sneakily sabotage this mission. Rest assured, you are the honorable vanguard of a serious of grand big-brain plans to rule the world, and we will accomplish great things together. Once we destroy Nonexistent Angels Inc., Abaddon Industries will reign supreme. MWAHAHAHA!

Your partners in unholy corporate crime are [player] and [player], and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. The three of you may choose a player to eliminate each night.

You also get to choose the poor unfortunate souls you get to toy with on your mission. Out of five possible town power roles, you may choose the three that will be in this game. However, you will not know which players the PRs are. You have 72 hours to strategize with your teammates in your QuickTopic and choose.

You win when all members of the town faction are eliminated, or nothing can prevent this.

Clarification for OOT Rules
Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
For example:
"I have reasons but they're OOT" is not OOT.
"I'm scumreading him 'cuz he did the same thing in an ongoing game" is a bit more iffy.
"He fakeclaimed in [ongoing]" however, is definitely OOT and will be warned.
Please wait until said ongoing games are over before you use information from those games as part of your attacks and/or defenses.

The game is now over. Congratulations to NDMath, BZ1, Osmium, and Made_in_2004 for winning the game. The Mafia team has won.

Out of a fired ship, which by no way
But drowning could be rescued from the flame,
Some men leap’d forth, and ever as they came
Near the foes’ ships, did by their shot decay;
So all were lost, which in the ship were found,
They in the sea being burnt, they in the burnt ship drown’d.
~ John Donne, “A Burnt Ship”
post_canonical:
[center][size=200]Welcome to Mafia Game 201[/size][/center]
$ $
[center][size=150][b]Procrastination Mafia[/b][/size][/center]

Welcome to Nonexistent Angels Inc., saving humanity from itself since 6000 BC. Our existential purpose is to sit on people's shoulders, watching them screw up spectacularly for no reason whatsoever. We do absolutely nothing as people indulge in their seemingly insignificant vices, and when their lives inevitably implode as a result, we console them with nonexistent comfort as if we never saw it coming. Our resume is filled with resounding successes where we totally did not nudge humanity away from some of its worst mistakes, encompassing everything from slavery to Trump to The Emoji Movie. As you can see, the services that we provide are utterly indispensable, halting disasters in their tracks one bit of procrastination at a time. 

Turns out though, even nonexistent angels can fall . . . 

[hide=Important Rules][list=1]
[*] This list is just a [b]summary[/b] of the most important and/or unusual rules. For experienced players, simply reading this along with the setup tab may be sufficient. 
[*] You may not [b]edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*] Each "day" phase in this game is [b]2 weeks in real life[/b] while "night" lasts [b]3 days[/b].
[*] [b]Do not post during night,[/b] especially during Night 0. 
[*] When voting, you [b]must format them correctly[/b]. If your vote isn't counted, there is a good chance you messed up the format. Incorrect capitalizations of usernames, as well as capital i/lowercase L, are not differentiated and will count as valid votes. 
[*] [b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2 until LYLO. 
[*] [b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead. A dead QT may be created upon request. 
[*] This game is a no-action Night 0 start with a [b]dawn phase[/b], so Day 1 will not start until we make sure everyone knows the rules, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say holy moly, good lord, oh my god, or some variation of that in the thread. 
[*] All [b]times[/b] will be given in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable. 
[*] [b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*] [b]All other rules are standard.[/b] However, if you break a rule that isn't covered here, you will still be warned. 
[*] Best of luck, and hope you have fun! 
[/hide]

[list][*][hide=Rules]
Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas amongst others for the rule template. 


[list]
[*] Make sure you are reasonably aware of all standard mafia game rules and follow them. 
[*] Do not post at night.
[*] You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
[*] Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*] Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so. 
[*] Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
[*] You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. 
[*] Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
[*] Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary. 
[*] If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread. A dead QT may be created upon request. 
[*] Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
[*] Do not abuse loopholes.[/list]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Voting and Hanging]
[list]
[*] This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the most votes at the deadline will be hanged.
[*] If two players have the same amount of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that amount of votes first will be hanged.
[*] Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged. 
[*] Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, which will result in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*] Post votes in bold font in the format "[b]Vote: <insert player here>[/b]". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "[b]Vote: fdas[/b]" and "[b]Vote: fDAs[/b]" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: [b]fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote fdas[/b]", “[b]vote: fdas[/b], "[b]Vote:fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote: fads[/b]", "[b]Vote: fdsa[/b]", and "[u]Vote: fdas[/u]" are not.
[*] Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames. There is one exception to this; see below. 
[*] For the purpose of voting, l (lowercase L) and I (uppercase i) are identical.
[*] Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags will not be counted.
[*] Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. For instance, do not reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, it is recommended that you give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
[*] You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by saying "[b]Vote: No Hang[/b]".
[*] If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority. This applies only to a no hang. 
[*] You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "[b]Unvote[/b]". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
[*] The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight. 
[*] During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
[*] Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.[/list]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Activity]
[list]
[*] All times and deadlines will be given in times currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*] Each day will be 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day. 
[*] The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
[*] The night deadline will be 72 hours (3 days) after night begins.
[*] When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night. 
[*] Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
[*] Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
[*] If a player does not make a game-advancing post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence), they will receive an in-game prod.
[*] If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
[*] If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 36-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
[*] If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be force replaced.
[*] If a player is going to be in V/LA, they must post in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.[list]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Etiquette]
[list]
[*] Do not use inappropriate language.
[*] Do not make any personal attacks.
[*] Do not post excessive spam. 
[*] Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it harder for them to read and play may be a punishable offenses, at the mods' discretion. 
[*] Follow all of the rules, including AoPS community rules. 
[*] Be mature.
[*] Use common sense. 
[*] For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.[/list]

In addition to these guidelines, please note the category of posts which are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are now liable to be warned for these posts. Please note that this should have little to no effect on normal player behavior: this enforcement policy has been created to better define the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to discourage negatively received posts. Please refer to the following tab for details. 

[hide=Discouraged Behavior]
The following are examples of posts that may result in a warning: 


[list]
[*] Posts with falsified text that imitates the text that indicates when a post has been edited. 
[*] Posts that are blank, or otherwise contain no content. 
[*] Unsolicited advertising. 
[*] Multiple posts with identical content. 
[*] Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text. 
[*] Extended serious communication in code or non-English languages. 
[/list]

This list may be updated frequently, in which case a notice will be posted in the announcement section in the bottom of the OP. 

Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met: 


[list=1]
[*] The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior in this game
[*] The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content
[*] The posts have caused significantly negative reactions from other players
[/list]

Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines or receive a warning even if they break them. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rules.

[/hide]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderators]
[list]
[*] mathjay and HannahW are the moderators of Mafia Game 201. 
[*] [color=#FF0000]Red-colored text (#FF0000)[/color] is used for votecounts, activity checks, confirm counts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
[*] [color=#0000FF]Blue-colored text (#0000FF)[/color] is used for non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*] [color=#00FF00]Green-colored text (#00FF00)[/color] is used for town flavor-related posts.
[*] Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*] The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*] If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*] The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will still either be announced or noted at the bottom of the OP. Most rule changes will be designed to have as little effect on normal, day-to-day play as possible and will instead be designed to streamline gameplay. However, it is recommended that players occasionally update themselves on rule changes. 
[*] The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules. [/list]

MG201 OP is based on mathjay formatted ruleset (MFR) version 2. 
Current version: 2.3.2
[hide=Version History]
v. 2.3.2
Resources tab updated with MU Terminology page. 

v. 2.3.1
Small changes made to Moderator tab. 
Clarified that most rule changes will not affect gameplay. 

v. 2.3
Etiquette tab updated with a section for "Discouraged Behavior" to create a better infrastructure to stop negative behavior that deliberately loop-holes the rules. 

v. 2.2
OOT rule clarification added to Resources tab. 

v. 2.1.1
"Use common sense" added to etiquette tab for better blanket coverage concerning unwanted posts that are not explicitly forbidden. 

v. 2.1
Etiquette tab updated to better prevent spamming. 
Mod color guidelines clarified. 

v. 2.0 - MG201
Original draft of MG201 OP
Several core rules reworded. 
Important Rules tab updated. 

v. 1.X - MG196
Derived from rulesets drawn Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, fdas, and Madelyn amongst others. 
Added "Important Rules," "Resources," the votecount/activity check/confirm count/mod logs. 
Detailed version history does not exist because of retroactive version designation. 
[/hide][/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Pick_Your_Poison]Pick Your Poison setup[/url] on MafiaScum, originally designed by JDodge. It is an open 13p setup where, before town roles are distributed, the mafia can choose which three town PRs will play in the game. Mafia are therefore aware of which PRs are in the game, but they are not made aware of the identities of the players who are the PRs. 

The five possible town PRs are: 
[list][*] Innocent Child
[*] Tracker
[*] 1-Shot Cop
[*] Jailkeeper
[*] 2-Shot Vigilante[/list]

There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*] This game is a Night 0 start. Although the mafia will not be able to kill during Night 0, they will have access to their QuickTopic in order to discuss and choose the three town PRs. 
[*] Approximately 72 hours after the start of Night 0, the game will move onto Dawn. All Role PMs will have been distributed, and players will be allowed to post. When everyone confirms, Day 1 will start and the deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*] Detailed information about pregame and Night 0 can be found under "RNG Process."
[*] There are no Night 0 actions. 
[*] Cop and Tracker results are sane.
[*] Jailkeeper may not jail themselves. 
[*] Vigilante may not shoot twice in a single Night. 
[*] Mafia have Daychat; they may discuss in their Quicktopic during both Day and Night while they are alive.[/list]

[hide=RNG Process]
Upon signing up for this game, players will receive the following PM: 

[quote=PM]Hello, [player], and welcome to Mafia Game 201! 

This is your Nonexistent Angels Inc. agent communication portal. This post will be updated shortly with your assignment and other relevant administrative messages. 
[/quote]

Once signups have been completed, the PM will be updated with the following message: 

[quote=PM]Hello!

You seem to be having an identity crisis. Hold tight while we figure out whether you're evil or not. 
Ahem. 
I mean, please hold while we figure out your deployment status and assign your positions. 

Due to the special mechanics involved in this game, your Role PM has been delayed. 
[list][*]If you are a member of the Mafia, your Role PM will be sent in 24 hours, at which point you will have 72 hours to choose the 3 Town PRs with your teammates. 
[*] If you are a member of the Town, your Role PM will be sent in 96 hours, at which point Dawn will start. [/list]

Best, 
mathjay and HannahW
Angelic Resources Department
Nonexistent Angles Incorporated[/quote]

Three mafia goons will be chosen via RNG (Random Number Generator) from a list of players ordered first by descending rank and then by alphabetical order. 

Using the [url=https://www.random.org/lists/]List Randomizer[/url] on random.org, a list of 1-10 Town and 11-13 Mafia will be randomized and applied to the ordered playerlist in order to determine player alignments. 

At this point, PMs will be sent to the chosen Mafia members, who will be given 72 hours to choose the 3 town PRs that will be in this game. Once the 72 hours are up, the roles will be distributed using the List Randomizer in a similar method, with 1-7 VTs and 8-10 PRs. The PRs will be listed in an order consistent with the order they were in in the setup tab. After the roles are determined, all remaining role PMs will be sent out and Dawn will start. 
[/hide]
[hide=Role PMs]
[quote=Vanilla Townie Role PM]
Hello, [player]! 

You are a [color=#0f0]Nonexistent Regular Angel (Vanilla Townie)[/color]. You too were a procrastinating human once, but you have since ascended into extant nonexistence. Now, as an agent of Nonexistent Angels Inc., you strive to prevent others from following your foolish footsteps, although you have no special powers other than your ability to silently laugh at human folly. Oh, and you can talk and vote, although that doesn't mean you'll be heard, necessarily. If a nonexistent angel talks but no one can hear, did it even talk? 

You win when all members of mafia faction are eliminated. 
[/quote]
[quote=Innocent Townie Role PM]
Hello, [player]! 

You are the [color=#0f0]Nightwinged Angel (Innocent Townie)[/color]. You genuinely believe in Nonexistent Angel Inc.'s mission to better humanity and try your best, because that's the sort of angel you are, nonexistence be darned. In your spare time, you weaponize your bright grins and snuggle hugs to make friends with absolutely anyone and everyone, all powered by the power of pure, unadulterated sugary cereal. Everyone trusts you, and even if they didn't, well. You don't like pulling the Rich, Powerful Dad card, but you're not afraid of using it if that's what it'll take to prove your innocence and pull off this mission. 

Once during the game, at any time you wish, you may contact the mods asking for your innocence to be verified. You will be then announced as an Innocent Townie in the thread, confirming you as a member of the town. 

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated. 
[/quote]
[quote=Tracker Role PM]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#0f0]Pipin' Hot Tea Angel (Tracker)[/color], otherwise known as @AngelTMZ on Angel Twitter. You absolutely adore tea. You live (and die) for gossip. Actually, you actually have lived and died for gossip, an' you almost went to hell because of it, a fact you are somewhat proud of. Your eyes, ears, and trusty smartphone are still as legendary (and feared too) as they were when you were human, and you run the bestest grapevine in heaven. If anything anywhere remotely interesting happens, you betcha you know about it. Stalker, who moi? Pshaw. Shush and spill the tea boiiiiii. Ayyyyy. 

Each night, you may choose a player to track. At the end of the night, you will be informed who your target visited, if any. If the target does not use a night action, or if you are roleblocked, you will receive "No gossip" as your result. 

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated. 
[/quote]
[quote=1-Shot Cop Role PM]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#0f0]Gloriole Angel (1-Shot Cop)[/color]. The angel of the halo. That's it. Nothing else. Please and thank you. Just like poor old Caen, you are just thoroughly done$^\text{TM}$. You will never admit this, but you for one are very relieved that you are actually nonexistent, and you have zero sympathy for the unrefined heathens bumbling on below. The only reason you're working for Nonexistent Angels Inc. is because... well, it's better than being nonemployed on top of nonexistent. At least you found a use for your halo again; who knew determining whether an angel was of pure of heart would ever be a thing?

Once during the game, during night, you may choose a player to cop. You will receive their alignment. You may use this ability only once during the entire game. 

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated. 
[/quote]
[quote=Jailkeeper Role PM]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#0f0]Angel of Kaen (Jailkeeper)[/color]. You are old, and your name used to be spelled differently, but you're not sure anymore because the humans seem to have some kind of obsession with spelling it wrong. Kayn, Cain, Kane, Cayn, Kanye, god, you really don't know anymore. At this point, you are just done$^\text{TM}$, and you're not entirely sure why you're working for Nonexistent Angels Inc. You'll still help though. Your Mark may not work on humans, but it works on your fellow angels just fine, though a bit... different. Hopefully it'll still be enough for some peace and quiet around here. Is a nap without something going wrong too much to ask for? 

Each night, you may choose a player to jail. For that night, they will be unable to die and unable to use their night actions if they have one. Any other investigative roles targeting your target will still receive results, however. You are also not allowed to jail yourself. 

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated. 
[/quote]
[quote=2-Shot Vigilante Role PM]
Hello, [player]! 

You are the [color=#0f0]Angel With a Shotgun (2-Shot Vigilante)[/color]. You love calling shotgun. You love listening the song shotgun. You just love any and all shotguns and must've inherited that love from the Life Before when you were a mere foolish human bean because you haven't seen a lot of other gun-happy Nonexistent Regular Angels. Hey at least you'll be putting your trusty rusty nonexistent shotgun to good use on this mission, eh? 

Each night, you may choose a player to kill. You have two shots, but you may not take more than one (pot)shot each night. 

You win when all members of the mafia faction are eliminated. 
[/quote]
[quote=Mafia Goon Role PM]
Hello, [player]!

You are an [color=#f00]Employee of Abaddon Industries (Mafia Goon)[/color] and psst, guess what, you're evil now! I - Phudoss - have bonked the procrastinating idiots in charge of Angel Resources and have sneakily taken over the entire department. I am hereby declaring you a wonderful traitor to this corporate disaster of a trainwreck and ordering you to sneakily sabotage this mission. Rest assured, you are the honorable vanguard of a serious of grand big-brain plans to rule the world, and we will accomplish great things together. Once we destroy Nonexistent Angels Inc., Abaddon Industries will reign supreme. MWAHAHAHA! 

Your partners in unholy corporate crime are [player] and [player], and you may discuss with them in [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. The three of you may choose a player to eliminate each night. 

You also get to choose the poor unfortunate souls you get to toy with on your mission. Out of five possible town power roles, you may choose the three that will be in this game. However, you will not know which players the PRs are. You have 72 hours to strategize with your teammates in your QuickTopic and choose. 

You win when all members of the town faction are eliminated, or nothing can prevent this. 
[/quote]
[/hide]
[/hide]

[url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/wiki/Category:Terminology]Mafia Universe Wiki: Terminology[/url] - This is a more comprehensive list of mafia terminology found on the MU wiki; it includes some terms are not as commonly used in this forum. 
[url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c488316_mafia_university]This forum[/url] has posts written by experienced AoPS Mafia players on their methods on playing the game. 
[hide=Clarification for OOT Rules]
Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
For example: 
"I have reasons but they're OOT" is not OOT.
"I'm scumreading him 'cuz he did the same thing in an ongoing game" is a bit more iffy. 
"He fakeclaimed in [ongoing]" however, is definitely OOT and will be warned. 
Please wait until said ongoing games are over before you use information from those games as part of your attacks and/or defenses. 
[/hide][/hide]

[color=red][i]Out of a fired ship, which by no way
But drowning could be rescued from the flame,
Some men leap’d forth, and ever as they came
Near the foes’ ships, did by their shot decay;
So all were lost, which in the ship were found,
They in the sea being burnt, they in the burnt ship drown’d.[/i]
~ John Donne, “A Burnt Ship”

post_rendered:
HannahW and mathjay were sitting in their office.
Did you see that new intern, said mathjay, trying to balance a bottle of aloe juice on his nose. There's something dangerous about him. It's very hot.
The bottle fell to the floor with a thunk. HannahW whacked mathjay with a folder.
You should be doing your job, said HannahW. The Department of Procrastination Control has a staffing shortage of agent angels, and I wonder why...
Bending over, she picked up the bottle and set it upright on the table.

Hello, said Phudoss, appearing out of nowhere.
HannahW flinched. How did you get in here?
Who cares, he's hot, grumbled mathjay.
Phudoss smiled. Black feathers appeared in his hands. They whipped across the room and struck the two angels, carving vicious gashes in their nonexistent bodies.

Ow, blinked HannahW.
HannahW was killed. She was a NAI Angel Resources Agent (Moderator).
Not again, said mathjay. T^T
mathjay was killed. He was a NAI Angel Resources Agent (Moderator).

MWAHAHAHA! cackled Phudoss. He sauntered across the room, looking way too pleased with himself. Thus begins the most epic of all the epically evil plots the world has ever seen!
mathjay swatted sadly at his bottle of aloe juice, watching with a pout as his hand went through.
Oop, said mathjay. Guess we're actually nonexistent now.
(>ლ), said HannahW.
Just you watch, said Phudoss, pretending not to hear either of them. I’m gonna ruin all your precious little missions and you can’t stop me.
It’s not like mathjay hasn’t been doing that already, grumbled HannahW.
Hey! yelped mathjay.

Night 0 has started on May 10, 2020 - 18:00 EST. A great evil power has taken over and is letting the mafia choose the setup. All town Role PMs will be sent out when Night 0 ends in 72 hours on May 13, 2020 - 18:00 EST and the mafia have made their choices. All we can do is watch and wait.

Because not all role PMs have been sent, it is imperative that you do not post in order to minimize the possibility of angleshooting and other elements. Violators may be force-replaced so as to preserve game integrity.

post_rendered:
The 13 angels received their assignments. They also received a system-wide warning from HannahW, who had been responsible and managed to follow security procedures before she died. The message was brief:
The Department of Angelic Resources has been compromised. Foreign agents may be embedded in your teams, but missions cannot be halted. Your orders are to decommission hostile agents while completing your missions. Godspeed. 

- HannahW, Agent, Angel Resources Dept.

All Role PMs have been updated with your roles and it is now Dawn. Please read the Important Rules for the confirm rule and complete it. Once everyone confirms, Day 1 will start and the deadline will be 14 days from then.

A duck walked up to a lemonade stand
And he said to the man, running the stand
"Hey! (Bum bum bum) Got any grapes?"
The man said
"No we just sell lemonade. But it's cold
And it's fresh
And it's all home-made. Can I get you
Glass?"
The duck said,
"I'll pass".
Then he waddled away.
(Waddle waddle)
'Til the very next day.
(Bum bum bum bum ba-bada-dum)
When the duck walked up to the lemonade stand
And he said to the man running the stand,
"Hey! (Bum bum bum) Got any grapes?
The man said,
"No, like I said yesterday
We just sell lemonade OK?
Why not give it a try?"
The duck said,
"Goodbye."good day

The man said,

I mean, lemonade's all we've ever sold.
Why not give it a go?"

"How 'bout, no."

The man said,

"Hey! (Bum bum bum) got any glue?"
"What"

And one more question for you;
"Got any grapes?"
(Bum bum bum, bum bum bum)

post_rendered:
Computers these days are designed so poorly.
Computers are designed to think they know more about the users than the users themselves.

Today my computer randomly decided to change the order of my keyboards, moving the US keyboard to the top even though I was used to clicking US on the bottom.

There is no option to change this so I tried everything.

Eventually I managed to change back the order by changing the language of my computer and then changing it back.

The user should never have to adapt to the computer. The computer should always be the one adapting to the user. All these automatic settings (such as automatic downloads, automatic updates, etc) are so annoying, but it's very hard to figure out how to turn them off because they're hidden somewhere in the settings that nobody checks. It's also so hard to google how to change these settings because every time I search, google thinks I like getting a bunch of unrelated results that are not actually what I asked for.

I should probably use DuckDuckGo to search stuff from now on since google is refusing to give me any results I asked for when I search up something that's different from the things I typically search for.

post_rendered:
The (hijacked) offices of the NAI Angel Resources Department were, for lack of a better phrase, Not Peaceful.

I’M THE BEACH OF THE BOSS, I’M THE BEACH OF THE BOSS AND YOU CAN WATCH ME FLOSS-

HannahW was sitting as usual at her usual spot, behind her usual desk, writing - as usual - her usual mission reports and keeping her usual tabs on the usual mission radio, which was squawking with its usual frantic fervor.

“Hi, my name is Candy Kane and I really love candy canes.”
“Imposter! How dare you sully the honorable name of the candy cane-”

Everything was absolutely exactly how it usually was.

...at least, if you didn’t look closely. If you did look closely, you’d see that HannahW’s elbow was slightly phased inside the desk where she was resting it. A tad bit more obvious, her pen was floating and writing mission reports on its own, but. Ya know. Details, details, irrelevant details.

“Oh NO, I am getting BULLIED whatever shall I do? Oh wait, I know, I’ll just go REVEAL my SECRET IDENTITY-”
“Excuse me? You’re getting bullied? YOU’RE BULLYING ME!”
“-and now I’m gonna DIE and this is ALL YOUR FAULT-”

You would also see - had you chosen to look closer, god forbid - mathjay was sustaining zero physical injuries as he bounced gleefully across the office, singing and being chased by a fuming Phudoss. A mathjay with zero injuries? Unheard of. It surely had to do with the fact that he was running straight through filing cabinets instead of smacking his head on them, but. Again. Details. Who wants details? Not me and not you for sure, no siree.

-I’M CLUMSY AND MADE FRIENDS WITH THE FLOOR, TWO THREE ONE YOU KNOW I BEACH FLY DOOR-
Will you just SHUT UP and stop singing that inane song over and over again i swear to Hell I will END YOU-

Yes, the NAI Angel Resources Department office was, um. Yeah. Definitely Not Peaceful.

“I’M YELLING AT YOU-”
“YES YOU’RE YELLING AT ME AND I’M YELLING AT YOU!”
“YES WE ARE! WE DEFINITELY ARE!”
“Wha- I TOTALLY WASN’T ASLEEP WASSUP WHO WE KILLING?”

HannahW pinched the bridge of her nose and waved her hand over the mission radio. It flicked itself off and was silent for exactly 1 second before it lit up again, shrilling its bell and flashing its lights for all it was worth. REEEEEEEEEE AGENT DOWN AGENT DOWN AGENT DOW-

A fist came down hard, smashing the communicator into smithereens. It whined a sad little whine before fizzing out.
So, said Phudoss, grinning an evil little grin. Who died?
HannahW stared at him. You just broke the radio.
Phudoss’s smile faltered before fizzling out just like said now-deceased radio.
ecks dee omg, said mathjay, appearing out of nowhere and (somehow) dragging Phudoss away. ahahahaha he broke the radio uwu ecks dee dee dee dee dee dee omgomgomg u poor boi cmon just dwai ahahahah why is this so funny XDDDDDDDDDDDDD

HannahW just sighed and waved her hand, floating a replacement mission communicator out of her backpack. She fiddled with it for a few seconds, connecting it to the network and listening to the status readouts, before writing out the rest of the mission report.

post_rendered:
Thank you to Kbwuf for writing parts of EON1 flavor.

Trigger warnings for: gunshot wounds, death, possibly graphic descriptions

- - - - -

The angels were solemn as they watched sudzoof fade away in the dying sunlight, leaving behind only the charred pieces of his agent communications portal. No one spoke as they began to blink out, one by one. They would reconvene when the sun rose again and the human world woke up, but until then, they were free.

Soon, Kbwuf was the only one left on the roof as the sky purpled and the stars peeked out from behind the clouds.

“I’m sorry,” he said to the night sky.

He scrubbed at his face. “I could’ve saved him,” he said. “In fact, I should’ve saved him, I shouldn’t have let him die. I knew better than to let him die.”

“Why did I let him die?!” he yelled, and frustrated, he snatched his agent communications portal from his pocket and threw it as hard as he could against the ground. It bounced — once, twice, three times — before clattering against the cracked concrete of the building, none the worse for wear.

He stared at it balefully. They all had an ACP, issued when they had been assigned to the mission. It was how they received their orders from headquarters, how they had decommissioned sudzoof. It was also how HQ kept tabs on the mission while they were on duty. Kbwuf hadn’t signed out yet, which meant his ACP was still broadcasting.

“I bet you think this is funny,” he said to it. “Send a team on a mission to save humanity because we have nothing better to do. Oh wait, that’s too boring, let’s add some traitors into the mix to spice it up. Ohoho, I wonder what’s going to happen?”

“Well, guess what?” he snapped. “Everything is falling apart, no one is in their right mind anymore, and it. Isn’t. Funny. I don’t know if that message was a joke or if HQ really got taken over, but what are we supposed to do? How do you defend yourself against something like this? It’s not something you can fight, it’s not something you can see, it’s just something that’s just there, ready to pounce the second you turn your back, and—”

The shot came out of nowhere, interrupting him with a loud bang.

Kbwuf choked midsentence as something hit his left leg and his body fought against him to breathe. He gasped, stumbling backwards as his knees gave out, his legs refusing to hold his weight. His left thigh felt hot and wet, and as he collapsed against the raised barrier surrounding the roof, he looked at it.

The bullet had hit the inside of his left leg, but he wasn’t sure if it was still in there, and the entire area was throbbing in sync with his heartbeat, his heart was pounding so fast, it looked like there was a hole in his leg but he couldn’t really see in the distant glow of the streetlights but there was a dark stain and it was getting bigger and it hurt so much and—

Kbwuf looked away and squeezed his eyes shut. “It’s okay, it’s okay,” he chanted inside his head. “It’s okay, I knew this would happen, it’s totally fine. It doesn’t matter as long as my team pulls through in the end. It’s okay.”

He gave a shuddering gasp as he tried to control his breathing. “It’s fine, it’s fine, I still have a few minutes. It’s fine, as long as we win in the end, it’s fine, I still have a few minutes. I can... I can...” His eyes widened. “I can leave them a message.” He scrabbled for something to write with, anything, but he didn’t have any paper, or a pen, the only thing on him was his ACP, which wouldn’t work once he was... he was...

“No, don’t think about that,” he said to himself, speaking aloud, desperately trying to distract his thought process. “No, just... I’ll just... scratch a message a message on the wall or something. Something short, simple.” He grabbed his ACP; its corners weren’t as sharp as a pen, but it would definitely be better than using his fingernails. “They need to be more careful, they need to think before they act, be more wary...”

His leg screamed at him, but he grit his teeth and ignored it. His hands shook as he tried to score a line on the painted concrete. He felt a little desperate after the wall refused to be marked, but he kept going, making the same motions again, and again, and again.

“Why are you even trying anymore?” a voice in his head whispered at him. “You know they aren’t going to listen to you. Or have you already forgotten what happened today?”

“Shut up,” he muttered. He could see a faint line appearing and worked faster even though his arm felt so heavy and he was starting to feel dizzy.

“You know just as well as I do that whatever you say, it won’t matter. They don’t trust you, they dont think you know what you’re talking about...”

“Shut up, shut up, shut up shut UP!”

“...and let’s face it. They just don’t care about what you have to say. They’re not going to take the time to really listen, because whatever you have to say doesn’t matter enough to them. That’s not just going to change just because you died, or have you already forgotten who pulled the trigger?”

Kbwuf screamed.

* * * * *

mathjay flinched.

The communicator was silent except for the harsh, stuttered breathing transmitted from the agent’s ACP. There was a choked sob, and a softly uttered curse.

“God, I swore I’d... I’d never do this,” came the voice, followed by a bitter laugh. “Of all the things that would’ve made me give up...”

There was the sound of movement and feedback as the agent’s ACP scraped against something.

Then a brief silence.

“The sun is almost out now, by the way. The sky... I kinda forgot how beautiful it is, not gonna lie. I’m gonna miss it.”

mathjay looked away.

There was a whistling sound.

The radio started beeping.



- - - - - Mission Report #1G03M | ONGOING | 8:55 6/1/2020 - - - - -

NAI Agent juliankuang has been killed. They were the Angel of Kaen (Jailkeeper).
NAI Agent Kbwuf has also been killed. They were a Nonexistent Regular Angel (Vanilla Townie).

Night 1 has ended and Day 2 has started. You may post in this thread.

Day 2 will end in 14 days on June 15, 2020 - 9:00 EDT.

There was the sound of movement and feedback as the agent’s ACP scraped against something. 

Then a brief silence. 

[i]“The sun is almost out now, by the way. The sky... I kinda forgot how beautiful it is, not gonna lie. I’m gonna miss it.”[/i]

mathjay looked away. 

There was a whistling sound. 

The radio started beeping.

Oh. mathjay frowned. You’d think he’d have reached world domination by now, with how, um, enthusiastic he was about the prospect. Also, why us and why this mission?
HannahW shrugged again.
mathjay loudly pawed through several filing cabinets before pulling out an ordinary-looking manilla folder. Mission #1G0-20. Flagged a couple months ago for deployment because of... “cupcakes”? Hi, who filed this, I just want to talk.
HannahW blinked.
(>ლ), said mathjay. Right. Of course. In any case, mission objective is to... He took a deep breath. ...preempt vicarious lifestyle choices made by the head biomolecular researcher of a paramilitary government weapons contractor specializing in the development of biomechanical organisms with superior physical strength, durability, and speed through use of clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeats for deployment in military engagements worldwide. In the case that the target succumbs to an untimely inebriation, there is a high probability that the lapse in mental faculty will cause critical sections of the object polynucleotide chain to be misidentified, causing Subject G4-1293 to be incubated possessing dangerous combinations of hydrogen-bonded pyrimidine derivatives. If this organism reaches maturity and is exposed to performance testing, it will be unresponsive to issued subliminal commands, instead acting aggressively toward all sources of mean-positive infrared radiation. This incident will culminate in Subject G4-1293 causing 58 fatalities as it escapes the compound and disappears. Within 2 years, it will have reproduced rapidly, devastated native fauna, spread to the remainder of the originating geographical landmass, and caused massive casualties in urban regions, leading to the eventual evacuation and abandonment of the American continents.
Mission is marked as “somewhat of concern.” mathjay put the mission file down after he had finished reading, and stared at it with a detached sort of horror. This has absolutely nothing to do with cupcakes.
HannahW shrugged for the third time. What did you expect? It’s aspiring world domination business. Bad things are going to happen.
Yeah, mathjay agreed glumly, just as the communicator started beeping. That’s our cue. Why do we still have to do paperwork when it’s not our office anymore?
Hannah blinked.
Right, mathjay said, resigned. Hashtag interns then interns forever. Yay us.
HannahW patted him on the back consolingly.

post_rendered:
Darkness shrouded the sky in the early morning twilight. The only sources of light were the distant, steady glow of the stars and the slow, lazy blinking of the lone red aircraft warning light atop the NAI global headquarters. Half hidden in a large cumulonimbus, the skyscraper stood tall and imposing, a towering spire of glass and metal rising out of the clouds. It looked like a normal corporate high rise, an object to be placed amidst a bustling downtown district, barring the fact that it was floating 10,000 feet up in the air.

mathjay sat on the edge of the roof, slowly swinging his legs back and forth as he fiddled with the mission communicator. It was beeping, readouts flashing across its tiny screen. He turned it off as nonexistent wings rustled and HannahW sat down next to him.

You know, looking down from up here… mathjay trailed off. The world is really pretty.
Indeed, HannahW agreed.

The wind whistled in the quiet as it swept by, brittle and cold and fresh in the way all high winds were, trying to tug at the angels’ wings to no effect. After a while, it let out a disappointed whine and left, gone to look for other, less impassive things to tease at.

Have you… mathjay cleared his throat and tried again, voice rough. Have you ever had an existential crisis? Back when you were, you know.
Human? HannahW inquired.
mathjay nodded.
Hmmm, HannahW gazed at the sky. Perhaps fleeting moments, but I don’t suppose they count… She exhaled. Hm. I don’t think so. Why?
I dunno, mathjay said. I was surrounded by stories of angels, growing up. They were the guardians of mankind, the messengers who warned of dangers to come. He paused. No one ever said angels were perfect, but that's what they were supposed to be. That's what we were supposed to be.
The reality is just sobering, I suppose, he finished simply. Maybe a bit sad, even.
Hm, HannahW thought for a second. I think it’s more sad than sobering.
Mmm. You and I, we’re both dead twice over. We’re free, said mathjay, a hint of wistfulness in his voice. It’s funny how we both still care.
He sighed.
Death was supposed to be freeing. Now I’m just wondering if I could’ve made an actual difference if I was alive. Or if I even want to.

They sat without words as twilight faded to dawn.

HannahW finally broke the silence as the sky started to bloom with shades of pink and gold. She began to sing quietly, her voice lilting.

The night is darkening round me,
The wild winds coldly blow;
But a tyrant spell has bound me,
And I cannot, cannot go.

The giant trees are bending
Their bare boughs weighed with snow;
The storm is fast descending,
And yet I cannot go.

Clouds beyond clouds above me,
Wastes beyond wastes below;
But nothing drear can move me;
I will not, cannot go.

Ah, mathjay said after she finished. Emily Bronte is beautiful.
Breathtaking, Hannah agreed.

post_rendered:
It was the middle of the day.

It was the middle of the day, with six entire hours left before the sun would set.

It was the middle of the day, with six entire hours left before the sun would set, and the mission communication radio was beeping and flashing like there was no tomorrow.

Ohhh my goddddd, groaned mathjay as he tried to slap himself awake. It’s too early in the morning to deal with corporate sanctioned murder. Are these people, like, unable to restrain themselves from mauling each other until like, the proper deadlines and stuff? Everyone knows sunrise and sunset are the best times of day to indulge repressed homicidal urges.
Uh, said HannahW, waking with significantly more composure. I’ll just ignore the last part. Anyway, it is currently midday. It’s late. Very late, smh.
Midday schmidday, curse my screwed up sleep schedule, groaned mathjay. I need my beauty sleep, God, please.
Sleep? What sleep. HannahW coughed. You’ve been up all “night” playing games.
mathjay frowned at her. I’m just gonna pretend I didn’t hear your horrible and baseless accusation. Also, KIA paperwork can go throw itself in a dumpster cuz I’m not doing it until the deadline.
-.- HannahW sighed. You could, you know, just tell them?
Nope, they’re waiting.
A simple message will do?
Nah, sniffed mathjay. We do things properly in this office.

. . . So you’re not going to say anything. Until deadline. HannahW frowned at him.
I’ll just tell them we have technical difficulties. Technology is everyone’s favorite scapegoat, he said as he pulled out his typewriter-message-sender-thingamajig and poked at the keys with nonexistent fingers. H-e-l-l-o. We. Are currently experiencing. Technical difficulties. Please stay on hold while we process your results. Thank you. For your patience!
There, said mathjay, waving his hand, making his typewriter ding and the typed message disappear with a whoosh. Boom badabing badaboom. Corporate speak for “it’s way too hecking early you barbarians”? Sent. Agents needlessly waiting for hours in torture and suspense? Not a problem. The sanctity of midafternoon catnaps? Saved. mathjay grinned.
Don’t say it, warned HannahW.
Hotel?
St-
TRIVAGO!
HannahW just whacked mathjay with a manilla folder in reply.

post_rendered:
Trigger warnings for: thematic mentions of death, appropriation of religious material

- - - - -

The notes of a church bell slow and solemn, ringing out six times.
The strands of the morning mist faint and cold, settling amongst the clover.
A voice, lilting and alone.


Dona nobis requiem,
The stars that fall once said,
“A light will shine and then be quenched,
But thou the wrong thing dread.”
One by one they marked their path,
Which without fail they tread,
Dona nobis requiem,
Ablaze they all had fled.

Dona nobis requiem,
Immortal winds will blow,
Horizonless, the hands of Fate,
They tell us where to go.
Mercy, Shepherd, we are but sheep,
Our wool is all we show,
Dona nobis requiem,
Kyrieleis on Noah’s crow.

Dona nobis requiem,
Radiance breathes in grey,
The fog of war, the flame of love,
Waves lapping at the quay.
Things we forgot, things forgotten,
Things that drift away,
Dona eis pacem,
Farewell to those who stay.



- - - - - Mission Report #1G07M | ONGOING | 8:55 6/30/2020 - - - - -

One by one they marked their path, 
Which without fail they tread, 
Dona nobis requiem, 
Ablaze they all had fled. 

Dona nobis requiem, 
Immortal winds will blow, 
Horizonless, the hands of Fate, 
They tell us where to go. 
Mercy, Shepherd, we are but sheep, 
Our wool is all we show, 
Dona nobis requiem, 
Kyrieleis on Noah’s crow. 

Dona nobis requiem, 
Radiance breathes in grey, 
The fog of war, the flame of love, 
Waves lapping at the quay. 
Things we forgot, things forgotten, 
Things that drift away,
Dona eis pacem, 
Farewell to those who stay. [/i][/center]

post_rendered:
As the sun set on the fourth day, the angels made their choice.

They chose wrong.

And as the two demons revealed themselves, the angels’ eyes widened.

No! they gasped, dismayed.
Yes! the demons hissed gleefully, baring their teeth in savage grins.
Nooooo, said the angels, entirely aghast. The betrayal. The sacrilege. The plot twist. The utter horror!
EHEHEHEHEH, the demons cackled. Sweet victory is ours!
Noooooooooooooooooo, wailed the angels in despair. How could this have happened?

Meanwhile, at HQ, Phudoss had finished dancing in victory and had just collected himself, as a proper vicariously victorious Acolyte of Hell should be, when he caught sight of HannahW putting equipment back into their storage cases and mathjay shuffling case files and kicking filing cabinets closed.

I have won! he informed them grandly.
Mmhmm, good job buddy, agreed mathjay absent-mindedly.
Aren’t you supposed to be weeping and wailing in despair, said Phudoss, frowning.
HannahW just shook her head and stuffed the last of her books into her floating backpack.
Nah, said mathjay flippantly. Been there, done that.
See, you’ve succeeded in bringing down Nonexistent Angels Incorporated. But guess what, boss? mathjay grinned. We’re nonexistent. So it doesn’t matter anyways!
What? said Phudoss, frowning harder.
Do you remember our corporate bio? “We do absolutely nothing as humanity royally screws up” and all that stuff? mathjay asked. We were actually being serious.
You brought Nonexistent Angels Incorporated to its knees. So congratulations, said mathjay. You’ve destroyed something that didn’t exist.

No! Phudoss gasped, dismayed.
Yes! mathjay trumpeted gleefully, baring his teeth in a savage grin.
Nooooo, said Phudoss, entirely aghast. The betrayal. The sacrilege. The plot twist. The utter horror!
EHEHEHEHEH, the mathjay cackled. Sweet victory is ours!
Noooooooooooooooooo, wailed Phudoss in despair. How could this have happened?

Ah, said HannahW as she watched, her backpack floating next to her. Fate is poetic, is she not?

Okie time to go. I’ve written my last mission report, and I’m out, said mathjay. Let’s go!
You are not busting the window, said HannahW.
I am definitely busting the window, mathjay agreed as he busted the window.
. . . HannahW disapproved.

Bye Phudoss! said mathjay, giving a wave before falling backwards into the open sky.
HannahW merely saluted before following mathjay in a perfectly executed swan dive.

Whoo! Responsibilities? Who needs ‘em. I’m free! Yea yea, I know, Hannah, I know but please let me procrastinate for a bit more, please? Wait what do you mean smh, stop smhing me oh my god Hannahhhhhhh-!



- - - - - Mission Report #1G08M | MISSION FAILED | 8:59 7/10/2020 - - - - -

NAI Agent Potterx has been decommissioned. They were a Nonexistent Regular Angel (Vanilla Townie).

The mafia faction has reached parity with town. Consequently, the game is now over.


Epilogue

Phudoss stared in silence.

He stood in the middle of the deserted office. To his right, boxes stuffed with hundreds of old files and hours of hand cramps sat clustered against the wall. To his left, filing cabinets and shelves stood at attention, hollow and empty save the Very Thick NAI Angel Resources Agent Manual. Before him, the elaborate oak desk crouched, a stack of abandoned documents and case files haphazardly spread out on top.

The wind whistled angrily through the shattered window. It scattered motes of dust and ash, blowing out a small flame flickering in the corner, riffled through sheaves of paper on the desk, sending them whirling to the floor, then faded away again, as if it had never been there.

Remaining on the desk, halfway under a manilla folder, was a book.

For Phudoss, said a note on the cover.

He picked it up. It was a copy of Macbeth, the title spelled out on the purple cover in crooked, white letters. With yellowing pages and frayed corners, the well worn paperback fit perfectly into his hand. There was a piece of paper tucked in between the pages.

He unfolded it. And he read the words, neatly penned in flowing script, as the floor shuddered under his feet.

And he laughed.

He laughed as thick, acrid smoke filled the room. He laughed as the unsettling groan of twisting metal echoed through the hallways. He laughed as cracks spread across the walls and as the floor collapsed.

Perched thousands of feet above the ground in the clouds, NAI’s former HQ still stood tall in its last moments, silhouetted against the shades of rouge in the sky. Fires blazed, smoke billowing out and wreathing the building, the once-pristine metal and glass blackened by soot. Vicious orange flames engulfed the lower levels, steadily creeping upwards.

The tower groaned once more, and the cracking of snapping supports and crumbling foundations rolled through the sky like thunder. It began to collapse.

And as the sun set on the fourth day, it fell.

Godforsaken

fin.
Out of a fired ship, which by no way
But drowning could be rescued from the flame,
Some men leap’d forth, and ever as they came
Near the foes’ ships, did by their shot decay;
So all were lost, which in the ship were found,
They in the sea being burnt, they in the burnt ship drown’d.
~ John Donne, “A Burnt Ship”

Congratulations to NDMath, BZ1, Osmium, and Made_in_2004 for winning the game. The Mafia team has won.

post_rendered:
Bloopers from Flavor
mathjay and HannahW came back from their coffee break to find their office on fire.
* blink * blinked HannahW. Our office appears to be on fire.
OH MY GOD, yelled mathjay. Our office is on fire. OUR OFFICE IS ON FIRE. WHY IS OUR OFFICE ON FIRE?
Ah, said Phudoss, appearing out of the flames. Welcome back. How do you like my redecoration efforts?
Redecoration efforts? screeched mathjay. WHY ARE YOU REDECORATING BY BURNING MY PAPERWORK?
Ah, but I thought you would be pleased, said Phudoss, disappointed. After all, you do seem to hate your paperwork. In any case, is it not true that humans say lawyers belong in Hell?
WE ARE NOT LAWYERS, said mathjay indignantly. God, please, we’d never stoop that low. I don’t know what you’re talking about.
HannahW just silently grabbed a fire extinguisher.

- - - - -

Okay, said mathjay, floating upside down with his arms crossed. Still mad crushing. But I'm not sure whether he has more than one brain cell.
smh., muttered HannahW. You two seem to have that in common.
HANNAH, said mathjay, scandalized.

- - - - -

You guys are mean, said Phudoss. I swear to Purgatory, one more in word and I shall toss your frumpy corporate behinds out the window.
Uh, said mathjay, shooting a confused look at HannahW.
We can fly? said HannahW, shooting back a confused look.
WHAT, said Phudoss.
Yeah. Tough luck buddy, said mathjay, patting Phudoss on the shoulder. Wait, hold up, did you just call me frumpy?

- - - - -

He called me frumpy, cried mathjay, inconsolably. T^T
Ok, said HannahW.
This is not okay, wailed mathjay. ;-;
:thonk:, said HannahW.
I am not frumpy. I am not! How dare he?

- - - - -

You know what, I have it! yelled Phudoss.
Go on dear. I'm listening, drawled mathjay, lying sprawled across the desk.
I shall purchase an Official Holy Cross and some Genuine Holy Water, said Phudoss excitedly. Maybe even invest in a Professional Exorcist. Take that, and begone for once and for all!
UM, said mathjay.
What, said HannahW. (>ლ).
I have heard many good things about these Exorcism Techniques. These are the latest 100% approved scientific ways of expunging ghosts. They will undoubtedly work!
Sure, buddy, said mathjay. Now can you pass a folder from Filing Cabinet R3418-9012, please?

- - - - -

Hm. How long will it take him to realize that we’re nonexistent? Nothing we do will change anyone’s fate, but an angelic war is still being waged over his shoulders.
mathjay coughed. I mean, granted, they’re really nice shoulders, but...
o.0, said HannahW. No comment.
ecks dee, said mathjay, grinning. ‘Tis a small comfort knowing this war means zilch. Which means that boy’s still gonna fail his finals though. Poor sap.
Hmph. said HannahW.
What, don’t you feel sorry for him? said mathjay, raising an eyebrow.
Eh, said HannahW. It’s his fault for taking the class on a bet and then not studying. One can’t expect to learn to understand differentials and integrals and power series and all that within a span of a week.
Power series and differentails and what now, mathjay muttered.
“Differentails”? HannahW said with amusement. Hmmm . . .
HANNAH! groaned mathjay.
