Moderator Comments and Player Feedback
Written by HannahW

On Town: -.-
  1. Post #75 is the best post in this game:

    Read it, comprehend it, and apply it. At any rate, spam is bad. It derails the conversation. It hands scum the advantage. It is annoying. It is bad. And it is bad, etc, etc. It also gives the mods aneurysms. Further elaboration.
    This is blatantly false:
    cw357 #286 wrote:
    My unorthodox posting pattern is distinctly strategic. Emotionally charged posts have a significantly increased propensity to elicit alignment indicative responses.
    1. This is wrong.
    2. It makes you scummy.
    3. Makes scum appear relatively calm and towny.
    4. Gives scum free town credit.
    Proof: You yourself said several posts back:
    cw357 #222 wrote:
    Made_in_2004 seem mega-towny, calm and yet accusatory responses to computer gremlins but me be upset if no reads me see from madein2004. Goodbye bwahahahahaha
    Oh, and spam makes it hard to go back and reread/analyze.
  2. Do not claim unnecessarily: julian -.- Especially in this setup and especially this early, it helps scum significantly more than town.
    First off, town doesn’t even know the PR’s while scum does. Claiming practically confs you as a PR and tells maf to target you. Furthermore, town doesn’t even know if your claim is legitimate or not. They have no reason to believe you at all. In other words, you just did the maf’s job for them while granting town next to no information
    Kbwuf #269 wrote:
    juliankuang wrote:
    i am town jailkeeper
    prove or disprove
    STOP DOING THIS IN YOUR GAMES
    this could also be a clever way of him being scum and not having this role picked so its an easy claim
    either way d1 isn't the day to do it as scum or town so idk why tf u did it god the incompetene
    asdf334 #305 wrote:
    and honestly jk would be really dumb to try and claim now
    so i think julian is basically outed scum
    sudzoof #395 wrote:
    Honestly I'm just convinced that jk is just bluffing since it's the only way I can keep my sanity
    … and there hasn’t even been a night yet.
    ; asdf, pretty much the same thing
    Claiming should be your last resort when deadline is imminent. Here, deadline was still days away, and you were so caught up in yourself that you overestimated by far the pressure on you. Reads and votes can change, especially D1, over a single post, and the people pressured early in the day or midday often may not be a main hang candidate at the end of the day. Of course, had you continued to tunnel, things would have stayed the same, but if you had taken a step back, evaluated the situation, and duly changed your behavior...
    .
  3. Stop tunneling. Elaboration
    Required conditions for the tunneling:

Transparency Report
A transparency report consisting of moderator decisions during the game that may have impacted gameplay in a negative way have been compiled for player perusal. None of these incidents have been internally determined as gamebreaking or major breaches of moderator conduct; this report has simply been written for the sake of player-moderator trust and transparency. Please contact the moderators via a Private Message if you have further questions or comments.

Incident #1: Alignment Rerand

Alignments were reranded once during pre-game phase. A list of 1-10 town and 11-13 mafia was randomized. However, the initial rand had slots 12 and 13 as mafia. Partly under the impression that the rand had not worked, and partly thinking that even if it had, it hadn’t worked “properly” because of how similar it was to the original list, the list was randomized again.

In hindsight, this was an error, and I apologize. The fact that slots 12 and 13 had remained mafia did not mean the list was not randomized. The initial rand was unfortunately not saved, and no other rerolls were made; the second alignment randomization was the rand that was applied to the playerlist in this game. All other RNG procedures were followed as detailed in the Setup tab in the OP.

Incident #2: Inadvertently Disclosed Noncritical Player-Mod Communications

Sometime late Day 1 on May 25, a question was asked in thread about vigilante shot action resolution upon death by cw357. The exact same question had been asked by vig player asdf334 in their Role PM the previous day, and in answering cw357 public question, I mentioned that asdf334 had asked the same question.

My initial thought was that as asking that question was not something that would reveal a player’s alignment, my comment was acceptable. However, upon second thought, my comment was determined to be improper as it disclosed private player-mod communications. Within 5 minutes, the comment was edited out of the post in question, which is #1627 in the thread. I apologize for the oversight.

Incident #3: Consulted Kbwuf for flavor while still alive

On May 29, the first day of Night 1, after the vigilante kill on Kbwuf had been submitted, I asked Kbwuf for quotes and input on the flavor conditional on his death. At this point, Kbwuf’s death was not certain - the vigilante could have changed their NA, or the jailkeeper could have blocked the shot - but I added a disclaimer stating that the question was not NA-indicative, as I was looking for flavor material and/or characterization whether Kbwuf survived Night 1 or not.

My determination was that the possibility that Kbwuf could gain information from my actions was low enough that it would be safe to consult him for flavor.

Incident #4: Inquired kevinmathz about replacement while night actions were pending

Sometime during week 2 of Day 2, kevinmathz expressed concerns about time constraints and possibly replacing out. The solution that was reached involved him playing out the remainder of Day 2, with the understanding that he would replace out if he survived until Day 3.

However, shortly after the beginning of Night 2, it occurred to me that it might not be fair for those with night actions - specifically, considering the mafia factional kill - for kevinmathz’s pending replacement to be unannounced. After discussion, the decision was made to confirm with kevinmathz that he would replace out if he did not die, preemptively search for a replacement, and announce that said replacement would be taking kevinmathz’s slot starting Day 3. At this point, the vigilante kill had already been submitted, but the mafia factional kill was more uncertain; the determination of the modding team, however, was that kevinmathz would likely survive until Day 3. As a result, he was asked to confirm his intentions of replacing out, to which he replied that his situation had changed and would now be playing.

I do not think this constitutes a case of modspew or modbias, as I would have implemented the preemptive replacement procedure regardless of whether or not I knew kevinmathz would or would not die during Night 2. My main concerns were the hypothetical situations where the mafia team might choose to kill kevinmathz for player skil when he would have replaced out, or kevinmathz guessing that he was not going to die during Night 2 because I asked about his replacement status, and I believe the solution the modding team came to was reasonably fair, all things considered.
post_canonical:
[hide=Bloopers from Flavor]
mathjay and HannahW came back from their coffee break to find their office on fire. 
[b]* blink *[/b] blinked HannahW. [b]Our office appears to be on fire.[/b]
[b]OH MY GOD,[/b] yelled mathjay. [b]Our office is on fire. OUR OFFICE IS ON FIRE. WHY IS OUR OFFICE ON FIRE?[/b]
[b]Ah,[/b] said Phudoss, appearing out of the flames. [b]Welcome back. How do you like my redecoration efforts?[/b]
[b]Redecoration efforts?[/b] screeched mathjay. [b]WHY ARE YOU REDECORATING BY BURNING MY PAPERWORK?[/b]
[b]Ah, but I thought you would be pleased,[/b] said Phudoss, disappointed. [b]After all, you do seem to hate your paperwork. In any case, is it not true that humans say lawyers belong in Hell?[/b]
[b]WE ARE NOT LAWYERS,[/b] said mathjay indignantly. [b]God, please, we’d never stoop that low. I don’t know what you’re talking about.[/b]
HannahW just silently grabbed a fire extinguisher. 

- - - - -

[b]Okay,[/b] said mathjay, floating upside down with his arms crossed. [b]Still mad crushing. But I'm not sure whether he has more than one brain cell.[/b]
[b]smh.,[/b] muttered HannahW. [b]You two seem to have that in common.[/b]
[b]HANNAH,[/b] said mathjay, scandalized.

- - - - -

[b]You guys are mean,[/b] said Phudoss. [b]I swear to Purgatory, one more in word and I shall toss your frumpy corporate behinds out the window. [/b]
[b]Uh,[/b] said mathjay, shooting a confused look at HannahW.
[b]We can fly?[/b] said HannahW, shooting back a confused look.
[b]WHAT,[/b] said Phudoss.
[b]Yeah. Tough luck buddy,[/b] said mathjay, patting Phudoss on the shoulder. [b]Wait, hold up, did you just call me frumpy?[/b]

- - - - -

[b]He called me frumpy,[/b] cried mathjay, inconsolably. [b]T^T[/b]
[b]Ok,[/b] said HannahW. 
[b]This is not okay,[/b] wailed mathjay. [b];-;[/b]
[b]:thonk:,[/b] said HannahW. 
[b]I am not frumpy. I am not! How dare he?[/b]

- - - - -

[b]You know what, I have it![/b] yelled Phudoss.
[b]Go on dear. I'm listening,[/b] drawled mathjay, lying sprawled across the desk.
[b]I shall purchase an Official Holy Cross and some Genuine Holy Water,[/b] said Phudoss excitedly. [b]Maybe even invest in a Professional Exorcist. Take that, and begone for once and for all![/b]
[b]UM,[/b] said mathjay.
[b]What,[/b] said HannahW. [b](>ლ).[/b]
[b]I have heard many good things about these Exorcism Techniques. These are the latest 100% approved scientific ways of expunging ghosts. They will undoubtedly work![/b]
[b]Sure, buddy,[/b] said mathjay. [b]Now can you pass a folder from Filing Cabinet R3418-9012, please?[/b]

- - - - -

[b]Hm. How long will it take him to realize that we’re nonexistent? Nothing we do will change anyone’s fate, but an angelic war is still being waged over his shoulders. [/b]
mathjay coughed. [b]I mean, granted, they’re really nice shoulders, but...[/b]
[b]o.0,[/b] said HannahW. [b]No comment.[/b]
[b]ecks dee,[/b] said mathjay, grinning. [b]‘Tis a small comfort knowing this war means zilch. Which means that boy’s still gonna fail his finals though. Poor sap. [/b]
[b]Hmph.[/b] said HannahW. 
[b]What, don’t you feel sorry for him?[/b] said mathjay, raising an eyebrow. 
[b]Eh,[/b] said HannahW. [b]It’s his fault for taking the class on a bet and then not studying. One can’t expect to learn to understand differentials and integrals and power series and all that within a span of a week.[/b]
[b]Power series and differentails and what now,[/b] mathjay muttered. 
[b]“Differentails”?[/b] HannahW said with amusement. [b]Hmmm . . .[/b]
[b]HANNAH![/b] groaned mathjay. 
[/hide]

[hide=Transparency Report]
A transparency report consisting of moderator decisions during the game that may have impacted gameplay in a negative way have been compiled for player perusal. None of these incidents have been internally determined as gamebreaking or major breaches of moderator conduct; this report has simply been written for the sake of player-moderator trust and transparency. Please contact the moderators via a Private Message if you have further questions or comments. 

[b]Incident #1: Alignment Rerand[/b]

Alignments were reranded once during pre-game phase. A list of 1-10 town and 11-13 mafia was randomized. However, the initial rand had slots 12 and 13 as mafia. Partly under the impression that the rand had not worked, and partly thinking that even if it had, it hadn’t worked “properly” because of how similar it was to the original list, the list was randomized again. 

In hindsight, this was an error, and I apologize. The fact that slots 12 and 13 had remained mafia did not mean the list was not randomized. The initial rand was unfortunately not saved, and no other rerolls were made; the second alignment randomization was the rand that was applied to the playerlist in this game. All other RNG procedures were followed as detailed in the Setup tab in the OP. 

[b]Incident #2: Inadvertently Disclosed Noncritical Player-Mod Communications[/b]

Sometime late Day 1 on May 25, a question was asked in thread about vigilante shot action resolution upon death by cw357. The exact same question had been asked by vig player asdf334 in their Role PM the previous day, and in answering cw357 public question, I mentioned that asdf334 had asked the same question. 

My initial thought was that as asking that question was not something that would reveal a player’s alignment, my comment was acceptable. However, upon second thought, my comment was determined to be improper as it disclosed private player-mod communications. Within 5 minutes, the comment was edited out of the post in question, which is [url=https://aops.com/community/p15438845]#1627[/url] in the thread. I apologize for the oversight. 

[b]Incident #3: Consulted Kbwuf for flavor while still alive[/b]

On May 29, the first day of Night 1, after the vigilante kill on Kbwuf had been submitted, I asked Kbwuf for quotes and input on the flavor conditional on his death. At this point, Kbwuf’s death was not certain - the vigilante could have changed their NA, or the jailkeeper could have blocked the shot - but I added a disclaimer stating that the question was not NA-indicative, as I was looking for flavor material and/or characterization whether Kbwuf survived Night 1 or not. 

My determination was that the possibility that Kbwuf could gain information from my actions was low enough that it would be safe to consult him for flavor. 

[b]Incident #4: Inquired kevinmathz about replacement while night actions were pending[/b]

Sometime during week 2 of Day 2, kevinmathz expressed concerns about time constraints and possibly replacing out. The solution that was reached involved him playing out the remainder of Day 2, with the understanding that he would replace out if he survived until Day 3. 

However, shortly after the beginning of Night 2, it occurred to me that it might not be fair for those with night actions - specifically, considering the mafia factional kill - for kevinmathz’s pending replacement to be unannounced. After discussion, the decision was made to confirm with kevinmathz that he would replace out if he did not die, preemptively search for a replacement, and announce that said replacement would be taking kevinmathz’s slot starting Day 3. At this point, the vigilante kill had already been submitted, but the mafia factional kill was more uncertain; the determination of the modding team, however, was that kevinmathz would likely survive until Day 3. As a result, he was asked to confirm his intentions of replacing out, to which he replied that his situation had changed and would now be playing. 

I do not think this constitutes a case of modspew or modbias, as I would have implemented the preemptive replacement procedure regardless of whether or not I knew kevinmathz would or would not die during Night 2. My main concerns were the hypothetical situations where the mafia team might choose to kill kevinmathz for player skil when he would have replaced out, or kevinmathz guessing that he was not going to die during Night 2 because I asked about his replacement status, and I believe the solution the modding team came to was reasonably fair, all things considered. 
[/hide]

Additional Moderator Comments

General Townplay Analysis and Feedback
By mathjay

Here are a few of the biggest mistakes that Town made in this game that I think the players should learn from and try to avoid in the future. These mistakes involve remarkably rudimentary principles of mafia which all the players should have been well aware of, but the result of this game shows that something evidently went wrong somewhere.

One thing I would like to note is that this particular setup - 13p Pick Your Poison - has a 72% Town winrate on MafiaScum, one of the sites with the best scum players. In retrospective, it is not a very balanced setup, and is extremely townsided.

1. It is up to town as a whole to stop tunneling.

Due to the nature of scumreads in mafia and the nature of tunneling, it is important to realize that it can be borderline impossible to defend against someone tunneling you. For instance, once you’re attacked, your options tend to be a) try to prove the attacks on you are factually incorrect, b) try to explain the behavior that was pointed out as “scummy,” or c) move on and try to scumhunt. When you’re being tunneled, however, none of these attacks work. If you try a), they’ll just come up with more attacks against you or ignore you because they’re tunenling. If you try b), they’ll just be unconvinced by your “excuses.” If you try c), they’ll say that you’re trying to “redirect attention” or trying to avoid defending yourself.

It’s really a not great situation to be in, and it’s also potentially really bad for town: if the right people are being tunneled, it’s not that bad, arguably. But if the wrong people are being tunneled, then it can throw the entire game, like in this game.

Therefore, it is up to the rest of town to keep an eye out for incidents of tunneling and keep it in check. In this game, town utterly failed to do this. asdf334 and cw357 were tunneling sudzoof and Kbwuf - the two most experienced town players in the game - which meant they couldn’t stop the tunneling themselves. NDMath and Made_in_2004 - the other two most experienced players in the game - were scum, and therefore had zero incentive to stop town from tunneling town. Every other town member was complicit in the tunneling; the easiest way you can improve your town gameplay is by stopping antitown behavior like this, and not just passively watch it happen. Even if you agree with the scumreads, it’s my opinion that it’s better to stop the tunneling and take care not to tunnel yourself.

2. Keep track of and call out the lurkers and get them to contribute.

In this game, two members of the scumteam lurked. Hard.

Absolutely no one in this game called BZ1 and NDMath for their apathy and lurking. Part of the reason for this was probably the tunneling that happened during D1; but I also think that a lot of town just did not notice. In fact, in a deeply ironical turn of events, NDMath was the one who called BZ1 out for lurking and scumread them. This evidently means that something went very wrong, townplay-wise.

Please keep in mind that I am not saying that lurking is a definitive scumtell. It is not. It is, however, the job of town as a whole to notice when people are lurking, call them out for lurking, and try to get them to contribute. From there, you can try to determine whether they are scummy or not, and it’s easier to read people when they actually post. Remember that lurking is always the easy way out for scum; BZ1 and NDMath simply being allowed to lurk and coast by was part of what made the game relatively easy for scum. They should not have been allowed to lurk by town as a whole, and they should have been scrutinized and forced to contribute, maybe getting scumread in the process.

And although thankfully it wasn’t as prevalent in this game, town should try their best to avoid lurking for the very same reason stated above.

3. Do not claim as PR.

This is a very, very basic concept, but as PR, you have two jobs: 1, survive until you can be useful, and 2, be useful.

Therefore, you should never, ever claim PR unless there is absolutely no way out, except by claiming. Claiming should be an absolute last-ditch option. Simply getting scumread is never an optimal reason to claim; my personal recommendation for claiming as PR is when you are at L-3/2/1.

For obvious reasons - namely staying alive - PRs staying hidden for as long as possible is optimal. Even in a setup such as Pick Your Poison where the scum knows the setup, PRs claiming early is nonoptimal. And in this particular setup, the PRs chosen were the jailkeeper and vigilante, and both PRs should theoretically have no reason to claim until lategame. At the very least, they should not have claimed D1.

Unfortunately, both jailkeeper and vigilante in this game claimed extremely early, during D1, and from a moderator point of view, both claims were not necessary. I also seem to recall one of them blaming their attackers for forcing them to claim - this holds zero merit. As PR, you should play safely: it is your job to avoid the hang and the night kill, and if you find yourself in a difficult situation, the majority of the responsibility rests on yourself to keep yourself alive.

It is the unfortunate fact that comes with randing a PR, but if you fail to stay alive, you hand scum a significant advantage, as evidently happened in this game.

Either way, decent scum performance.

I would maybe work on giving more detailed thoughts on people, and what conclusions you drew, and on more people too, not just one or two, so that someone who read your posts could, at the very least, understand your thoughts and what you were thinking and what your reads were. I would also read the section above on being a responsible member of town, and think about it in your future games.
